Summary of Changes\n\nHello @Nishitbaria, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed!\n\nThis pull request implements a blog feature for the RunAgents marketing site, leveraging Basehub CMS for content management. It includes the necessary components, API routes, and configuration to fetch and render blog posts, with a focus on static generation and SEO best practices. Note that manual setup steps in Basehub are required before the blog will function correctly.\n\n### Highlights\n\n* **Basehub CMS Integration**: This PR introduces a new blog feature powered by Basehub CMS, allowing content to be managed directly within the Basehub dashboard.\n* **Static Generation**: The blog index and post pages are statically generated (`force-static` and `generateStaticParams`), ensuring optimal performance and SEO.\n* **Rich Text Rendering**: The PR includes a `RichText` renderer with custom element handlers for headings, code blocks, images, and links, providing a flexible content creation experience.\n* **Environment Variable Validation**: The `BASEHUB_TOKEN` environment variable is validated using T3 Env + Zod, ensuring that the application is properly configured to connect to Basehub.\n* **Manual Setup Required**: Before this PR can be fully functional, manual steps are required, including creating a Basehub account, defining the Posts collection schema, and setting the `BASEHUB_TOKEN` environment variable.\n\n🧠 **New Feature in Public Preview:** You can now enable **Memory** to help **Gemini Code Assist** learn from your team's feedback. This makes future code reviews more consistent and personalized to your project's style. **Click [here](https://codeassist.google/code-review/login) to enable Memory in your admin console.**\n\n<details>\n<summary><b>Changelog</b></summary>\n\n* **.env.example**\n    * Added BASEHUB_TOKEN to .env.example\n* **.gitignore**\n    * Added .basehub and basehub-types.d.ts to .gitignore\n* **basehub.config.ts**\n    * Added basehub.config.ts to set global configurations for Basehub.\n* **docs/prd-basehub-blog.md**\n    * Added PRD document outlining the Basehub CMS blog implementation.\n* **package.json**\n    * Modified package.json to include basehub as a dependency and update the dev, build, and postinstall scripts.\n* **pnpm-lock.yaml**\n    * Updated pnpm-lock.yaml to reflect changes in dependencies.\n* **src/app/(landing)/blog/[slug]/page.tsx**\n    * Added dynamic route for individual blog posts.\n* **src/app/(landing)/blog/page.tsx**\n    * Added blog index page.\n* **src/app/api/draft/route.ts**\n    * Added placeholder API route for draft mode.\n* **src/components/blog/avatar.tsx**\n    * Added Avatar component for displaying author avatars.\n* **src/components/blog/cover-image.tsx**\n    * Added CoverImage component for displaying blog post cover images.\n* **src/components/blog/post-body.tsx**\n    * Added PostBody component for rendering rich text content.\n* **src/components/blog/post-card.tsx**\n    * Added PostCard component for displaying blog post previews.\n* **src/components/blog/post-header.tsx**\n    * Added PostHeader component for displaying blog post headers.\n* **src/env/server.ts**\n    * Added BASEHUB_TOKEN validation to src/env/server.ts.\n* **src/lib/basehub.action.ts**\n    * Added server actions for fetching blog data.\n* **src/lib/basehub.fragment.ts**\n    * Added query fragments for Basehub data fetching.\n* **src/lib/basehub.ts**\n    * Added Basehub client initialization.\n* **tsconfig.json**\n    * Modified tsconfig.json to include basehub-types.d.ts and basehub.config.ts\n</details>\n\n<details>\n<summary><b>Activity</b></summary>\n\n* The pull request introduces a new blog feature powered by Basehub CMS.\n* It includes components, API routes, and configuration for fetching and rendering blog posts.\n* The implementation focuses on static generation and SEO best practices.\n* Manual setup steps in Basehub are required before the blog will function correctly.\n</details>\n\n<details>\n<summary><b>Using Gemini Code Assist</b></summary>\n<br>\n\nThe full guide
